title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: Guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working repository.

title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: monecascale sibling package moneca hosts scalable clustering backends mobility network analysis. extends moneca beyond classification-scale matrices (dozens hundred categories) toward settings person × employer mobility (10^6 × 10^5). Every exported entry point returns moneca-class S3 object, moneca’s analysis plotting stack consumes output unchanged.

title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: moneca: reference algorithm (moneca(), moneca_fast()), weight matrix (weight.matrix()), segment metadata (moneca_segments()), full segment.*() plot_moneca_*() consumer API. monecascale: Imports: moneca. Adds alternative backends whose output consumed moneca unchanged. Local path sister package: /Users/giampaolomontaletti/Documents/funzioni/MONECA/.

title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: "greed" (default, R-native, sparse dgCMatrix, scales ~104–105 nodes) "graphtool" (Python via reticulate; conda-forge install; scales 10^6+ nodes) moneca_sbm_install_graphtool() — conda-forge install helper graphtool backend.

title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: Landed unless otherwise noted. See ~/.claude/plans/monecascale-scaling-roadmap.md ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/scaling_directions.md. D1 moneca_bipartite() — rectangular person × employer input. D3 moneca_flow() — Infomap / Map Equation. D4 moneca_localclique() — quasi-cliques, k-clique percolation, local Personalized PageRank expansion. D5 moneca_nmf() — Poisson NMF. D6 auto_segment_levels() — auto-stopping criteria (MDL, stability, plateau). Composes moneca’s moneca_fast() — may partly live moneca. D7 moneca_duckdb() / moneca_arrow() — --core / streaming backend.

title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: R/monecascale-package.R — package-level roxygen + "_PACKAGE". R/moneca_sbm.R — public moneca_sbm() entry + backend dispatcher. R/sbm_backend_greed.R — R-native greed backend. R/sbm_backend_graphtool.R — Python/graph-tool backend + install helper. R/sbm_utils.R — shared preprocessing (margins, symmetry, diagonal zeroing) .sbm_fit_to_moneca() adapter. R/sparse_internal.R — local copies two internal moneca helpers (segment_matrix_sparse, sparse_pmin_symmetric). Avoid calling moneca::: package. tests/testthat/helper-test-data.R — shared generators wrapping moneca::generate_mobility_data(). vignettes/monecascale-sbm.Rmd — RR ≡ DC-SBM bridge walkthrough parity experiment.

title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: 2-space indentation. Section comments R scripts: # 1. name section -----, #### dividers. Explicit moneca:: qualification whenever calling moneca APIs — makes dependency crossings visible. moneca:::internal references. Copy internal helpers locally comment pointing upstream file. Variable naming: mixed Italian / English allowed match moneca domain (retribuzione, arco, chunk_id, overlap_flag). Italian prose must use properly accented characters (è, é, à, ò, ù, ì, perché, più). Primary docs English; Italian reserved translations. Neutral technical tone. Avoid emphatic phrasing.

title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: Every entry point must return list class "moneca" minimum: segment.list — list lists. segment.list[[1]] atomic (.list(1:n_core)). Levels ≥ 2 lists integer-vector cliques singletons pruned (length ≥ 2). mat.list — list matrices. mat.list[[1]] input margins; subsequent levels aggregated via matching membership vector. small.cell.reduction, margins_added, density_reduction, symmetric_method — scalars / flags mirroring moneca::moneca_fast(). segment_metadata — result moneca::moneca_segments() called otherwise-complete object. Direction-specific diagnostics (e.g. $sbm_diagnostics) go extra slot, never inside core slots .

title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: testthat (>= 3.0.0). Edition 3. Optional-backend tests must skip_if_not_installed("greed") skip_if_not(reticulate::py_module_available("graph_tool")). New directions add: core-behavior test file, parity / sanity test file, skippable backend test file optional dependency involved. Keep test fixtures small (n ≤ 20 categories unit tests; n = 20–50 parity fixtures).

what: Adding a new direction (D1 / D3 / D4 / D5 / D7)
title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: Create R/moneca_<direction>.R public entry backend dispatcher (mirror moneca_sbm.R). needed, add R/<direction>_backend_*.R per alternative backend. Reuse R/sbm_utils.R helpers applicable (especially .ensure_margins_sbm, .preprocess_for_sbm, .sbm_fit_to_moneca). adapter needs differ, extract shared core helper rather duplicating. Add tests/testthat/test-<direction>.R — core behavior + parity vs moneca::moneca_fast() sensible. Add vignettes/monecascale-<direction>.Rmd. Register new exports _pkgdown.yml Core Clustering Backends section; add vignette articles section. NEWS.md entry minor version bump (0.1.0 → 0.2.0 etc.).

title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: monecascale sibling package moneca. provides scalable clustering backends whose output consumed unchanged moneca’s analysis plotting stack. first backend, moneca_sbm(), replaces moneca’s clique-enumeration step hierarchical degree-corrected stochastic block model (DC-SBM) inference — natural statistical twin moneca’s relative-risk framing. vignette motivates design, shows minimal end--end workflow, reproduces empirical parity measurement moneca::moneca_fast(), explains reach backend.

what: The theoretical bridge: RR = O/E is the DC-SBM residual
title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: MONECA’s weight matrix relative-risk deviation observed mobility independence null: RRij=Oij(ri⋅cj)/T. RR_{ij} = \frac{O_{ij}}{\bigl(r_i \cdot c_j\bigr) / T}. degree-corrected stochastic block model (Karrer & Newman, 2011) models expected number edges nodes j \theta_i \theta_j \omega_{g_i, g_j}, \theta degree parameters \omega block-interaction matrix. Setting \omega identity yields exactly (r_i c_j) / T expected count — .e., MONECA’s independence null. sense MONECA’s RR DC-SBM residual, clique rule thresholded RR hand-rolled approximation block inference. modern DC-SBM inference routine therefore principled scalable drop-. Two backends wired behind moneca_sbm(): "greed" (default, R-native via greed::DcSbm): hierarchical dendrogram, Poisson likelihood counts, sparse dgCMatrix input end--end, portable parallel via future framework. Scales comfortably ~10^4 – 10^5 nodes. "graphtool" (opt-, Python via reticulate): Peixoto’s minimize_nested_blockmodel_dl(), uses merge-split MCMC minimize description length directly. Scales 10^6+ nodes commodity hardware.

title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: moneca::segment.membership.dataframe() provides tidy membership view moneca_fast() output:

title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: Unlike moneca::moneca_fast(), SBM backends return full nested hierarchy without requiring user pre-specify segment.levels. $sbm_diagnostics slot records number blocks MDL-analogue score every level: adapter preserves moneca’s convention: segment.list[[1]] atomic segment.list[[l]] l >= 2 runs finest coarsest. Passing segment.levels = 3 truncates hierarchy three approximately log-spaced levels preserving finest coarsest.
code: seg$sbm_diagnostics$n_blocks_per_level #>  [1] 17 16 15 14 13 12 11 10  9  8  7  6  5  4  3  2 round(seg$sbm_diagnostics$mdl_per_level, 2) #>  [1]  793.02  826.18  856.59  892.55  933.79  974.81 1019.72 1085.49 1206.58 #> [10] 1371.64 1589.59 1806.06 2027.65 2244.25 2592.20 3534.60 seg3 <- moneca_sbm(mob, backend = "greed", seed = 2026, segment.levels = 3) length(seg3$segment.list) #> [1] 4 lengths(seg3$segment.list) #> [1] 20  3  9  2

title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: theoretical bridge right, moneca_sbm(backend = "greed") recover segmentations close moneca::moneca_fast() well-structured mobility matrix. measure directly adjusted Rand index (ARI) normalized mutual information (NMI) per-node labelings produced methods. 20-class synthetic fixture best matching level pair typically recovers ARI ~ 0.67 NMI ~ 0.90 — ARI >= 0.55 threshold set aspirational target direction planned, strong empirical confirmation RR ≡ DC-SBM bridge. match exact — . Clique enumeration block-model inference structurally different procedures: cliques insist internal completeness threshold, blocks allow stochastically-consistent edge pattern within group. two agree segmentation, mobility data sharply modular block structure; disagree, discrepancy diagnostic.
code: ari <- function(a, b) {   tab <- table(a, b); n <- sum(tab)   sc <- function(x) sum(choose(x, 2))   a_sum <- sc(rowSums(tab)); b_sum <- sc(colSums(tab))   tab_sum <- sc(as.vector(tab))   expected <- a_sum * b_sum / choose(n, 2)   max_idx <- (a_sum + b_sum) / 2   if (max_idx == expected) return(1)   (tab_sum - expected) / (max_idx - expected) }  nmi <- function(a, b) {   tab <- table(a, b); n <- sum(tab); p <- tab / n   pa <- rowSums(p); pb <- colSums(p); nz <- p > 0   mi <- sum(p[nz] * log(p[nz] / outer(pa, pb)[nz]))   ha <- -sum(pa[pa > 0] * log(pa[pa > 0]))   hb <- -sum(pb[pb > 0] * log(pb[pb > 0]))   if (ha == 0 || hb == 0) return(0)   mi / sqrt(ha * hb) }  level_labels <- function(mon, level) {   n_core <- nrow(mon$mat.list[[1]]) - 1L   labels <- rep(NA_integer_, n_core)   cliques <- mon$segment.list[[level]]   for (b in seq_along(cliques)) labels[cliques[[b]]] <- b   na_idx <- is.na(labels)   if (any(na_idx)) {     labels[na_idx] <- max(0L, labels, na.rm = TRUE) + seq_len(sum(na_idx))   }   as.integer(labels) } fast <- moneca::moneca_fast(mob, segment.levels = 3, progress = FALSE) sbm  <- moneca_sbm(mob, backend = "greed", seed = 2026)  fast_levels <- seq.int(2L, length(fast$segment.list)) sbm_levels  <- seq.int(2L, length(sbm$segment.list))  grid <- expand.grid(fast_lvl = fast_levels, sbm_lvl = sbm_levels) grid$ari <- mapply(function(lf, ls) ari(level_labels(fast, lf),                                         level_labels(sbm, ls)),                    grid$fast_lvl, grid$sbm_lvl) grid$nmi <- mapply(function(lf, ls) nmi(level_labels(fast, lf),                                         level_labels(sbm, ls)),                    grid$fast_lvl, grid$sbm_lvl)  best <- grid[which.max(grid$ari), , drop = FALSE] best #>    fast_lvl sbm_lvl       ari      nmi #> 19        2       8 0.6709957 0.905422

title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: Rule thumb: use greed exceed roughly 5000 nodes, move graphtool. backend = "auto" default encodes rule falls back greed informational message graph-tool installed.

title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: graph-tool distributed PyPI, macOS homebrew formula compiles source breaks Boost/Python version bumps. supported route conda-forge. moneca_sbm_install_graphtool() wraps conda step: , moneca_sbm(backend = "graphtool") works identically "greed". typical install takes 2–5 minutes warm conda cache.

title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: Every moneca function consumes moneca object consumes monecascale::moneca_sbm() output unchanged:
code: moneca::segment.membership(seg)[1:6, ] #>      name membership #> 1 Class 1       17.2 #> 2 Class 2       17.2 #> 3 Class 3       17.2 #> 4 Class 4       17.2 #> 5 Class 5       17.2 #> 6 Class 6       17.2 if (requireNamespace("ggraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   moneca::plot_moneca_hierarchical(seg, title = "monecascale - greed backend") } if (requireNamespace("ggraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   moneca::plot_moneca_ggraph(     seg, level = 2, node_color = "segment",     title = "monecascale - finest level"   ) } if (requireNamespace("ggraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   moneca::plot_moneca_dendrogram(seg) }

title: monecascale: hierarchical DC-SBM backend for MONECA
text: moneca_sbm() first seven directions MONECA scaling roadmap. Known limits release: Rectangular input accepted. Person × employer matrices target use case direction D1 (moneca_bipartite()); moneca_sbm() currently errors non-square input pointer . Auto-level unification across backends (segment.levels = "auto" everywhere including moneca::moneca_fast()) direction D6. --core inference data fit RAM direction D7 (moneca_duckdb / moneca_arrow).

what: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: Author: Giampaolo Montaletti ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0002-5327-1122 Email: giampaolo.montaletti@gmail.com monecascale sibling package moneca provides scalable clustering backends mobility network analysis. designed extend moneca beyond classification-scale matrices (dozens hundred categories) toward settings person × employer mobility (10^6 × 10^5).

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: moneca remains home reference algorithm (moneca(), moneca_fast()), weight-matrix construction, segment metadata, full visualization stack. monecascale adds alternative clustering backends whose output consumed unchanged moneca’s analysis plotting functions. Every monecascale entry point returns moneca-class S3 object extra diagnostics slot.

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: Hierarchical degree-corrected stochastic block model (DC-SBM) clustering. Two dispatchable backends: "greed" (default, R-native via greed::DcSbm) — sparse dgCMatrix end--end, hierarchical dendrogram via cut(sol, k), portable parallel via future. Scales ~104–105 nodes. "graphtool" (opt-, Python via reticulate) — Peixoto’s minimize_nested_blockmodel_dl(), MDL-based level selection. Scales 10^6+ nodes. Theoretical rationale. moneca’s RR = O / E DC-SBM residual identity block interaction; DC-SBM inference therefore principled scalable drop-moneca’s clique step. Empirical parity 20-class synthetic fixture: ARI = 0.671, NMI = 0.905 vs moneca::moneca_fast().

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
code: # Install moneca first (required) devtools::install_github("gmontaletti/MONECA")  # Then monecascale devtools::install_github("gmontaletti/monecascale")  # Optional backend dependencies install.packages("greed")                              # default R-native backend install.packages("reticulate")                         # for graph-tool backend monecascale::moneca_sbm_install_graphtool()            # installs graph-tool via conda

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: See vignette("monecascale-sbm") RR ≡ DC-SBM residual walkthrough, parity experiment, guidance choosing backend.
code: library(monecascale) library(moneca)  mob <- generate_mobility_data(   n_classes = 20, n_total = 5000,   immobility_strength = 0.5, class_clustering = 0.75,   seed = 2026 )  seg <- moneca_sbm(mob, backend = "greed", seed = 2026)  seg$sbm_diagnostics$n_blocks_per_level segment.membership(seg) plot_moneca_hierarchical(seg)

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: monecascale home following scaling directions MONECA Scaling Roadmap: D2 — hierarchical DC-SBM (moneca_sbm, shipped). D1 — bipartite / two-mode (moneca_bipartite, planned). D3 — flow-based (moneca_flow via Infomap, planned). D4 — scalable clique guardrail (moneca_localclique, planned). D5 — Poisson NMF (moneca_nmf, planned). D6 — auto-level detection (auto_segment_levels, planned). D7 — --core (moneca_duckdb / moneca_arrow, planned).

what: Hierarchical DC-SBM Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_sbm
title: Hierarchical DC-SBM Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_sbm
text: Alternative moneca::moneca_fast() replaces clique enumeration hierarchical degree-corrected stochastic block model (DC-SBM) inference. MONECA's weight matrix RR = O / E expected-value residual DC-SBM identity block interaction, DC-SBM clustering principled scalable replacement clique step.

title: Hierarchical DC-SBM Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_sbm
code: moneca_sbm(   mx,   backend = c("auto", "greed", "graphtool"),   deg_corr = TRUE,   edge_model = c("poisson", "bernoulli"),   small.cell.reduction = 0,   symmetric_method = c("sum", "min", "none"),   segment.levels = NULL,   max_K = NULL,   isolates = FALSE,   has_margins = "auto",   seed = NULL,   n_init = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Hierarchical DC-SBM Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_sbm
text: mx square mobility matrix, optionally margins last row column (auto-detected via has_margins = "auto"). Sparse dgCMatrix input supported end--end greed backend. backend One "auto", "greed", "graphtool". "auto" prefers graphtool available matrix exceeds 5000 nodes; otherwise uses greed. deg_corr Logical. TRUE (default), fits degree-corrected SBM. Set FALSE plain SBM. edge_model One "poisson" (default, appropriate count-valued mobility) "bernoulli" (binary edges). small.cell.reduction Numeric. Cells core matrix value zeroed SBM inference. Mirrors moneca::moneca_fast() parameter. symmetric_method One "sum" (default; mx + t(mx)), "min" (2 * pmin(mx, t(mx)), -weights one-way flows), "none" (keep directed). Applied core SBM. segment.levels Integer NULL. NULL (default), hierarchy levels produced backend (K_final 2) returned. integer, hierarchy truncated many approximately log-spaced levels. max_K Integer NULL. Upper bound number blocks finest level (greed backend ). Defaults max(20, n_core / 5). isolates Logical. TRUE, attaches $isolates_summary slot mirroring moneca::moneca_fast(). has_margins One "auto" (default), TRUE, FALSE. Controls whether detect /append row/column margins input matrix. seed Integer seed reproducible inference. n_init Integer. Number independent backend runs; best-scoring fit retained. Defaults 1 greed (quasi-deterministic) 5 typical graphtool (MCMC). verbose Logical. TRUE, prints backend progress. ... Additional arguments forwarded backend.

title: Hierarchical DC-SBM Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_sbm
text: object class "moneca" structure   moneca::moneca_fast(), plus extra slot   $sbm_diagnostics describing backend used, number   blocks per level, MDL/ICL trace.

title: Hierarchical DC-SBM Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_sbm
text: number hierarchical levels selected automatically backend (ICL greed; MDL graphtool), eliminates MONECA's manual segment.levels requirement. Passing segment.levels truncates backend's full hierarchy many approximately log-spaced levels, preserving finest coarsest.

title: Hierarchical DC-SBM Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_sbm

title: monecascale 0.1.0
text: Sibling package moneca providing scalable clustering backends. Extracted moneca branch new_directions 2026-04-23.

title: monecascale 0.1.0
text: "greed" (default, R-native via greed::DcSbm): hierarchical dendrogram, Poisson likelihood, sparse dgCMatrix end--end, portable parallel via future framework. Scales ~104–105 nodes. "graphtool" (opt-, Python via reticulate): Peixoto’s minimize_nested_blockmodel_dl() automatic MDL-based level selection. Scales 10^6+ nodes. Install via moneca_sbm_install_graphtool(). output moneca_sbm() standard moneca-class S3 object consumed unchanged moneca::segment.membership(), moneca::segment.quality(), moneca::plot_moneca_*() etc. extra $sbm_diagnostics slot records backend, MDL/ICL per level, number blocks per level.

title: monecascale 0.1.0
text: moneca’s weight matrix RR = O / E structurally expected-value residual DC-SBM identity block interaction. Consequence: moneca_fast() approximately DC-SBM clustering done clique enumeration thresholded residual, modern DC-SBM inference (Peixoto’s graph-tool, greed) principled drop-.

title: monecascale 0.1.0
text: 20-class synthetic fixture (generate_mobility_data(n_classes = 20, immobility_strength = 0.5, class_clustering = 0.75, seed = 2026)), best-matching level pair moneca_sbm(backend = "greed") moneca::moneca_fast() reaches ARI = 0.671, NMI = 0.905 — 0.55 aspirational target.
